diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient was diagnosed with cobalamin C (cblC) disease during newborn screening prompted by poor feeding and jaundice. The diagnosis was confirmed by the identification of a homozygous duplication mutation (c.271dupA) in the methylmalonic aciduria and homocystinuria type C (MMACHC) gene. The patient exhibited methylmalonic aciduria with hyperhomocysteinemia, leading to the diagnosis. The retinal structure was examined, revealing a rapidly progressing bilateral maculopathy leading to atrophic macular lesions, consistent with cblC disease.
